scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced the packaging order through readiness, running, and completion states only when setup, traceability, reconciliation, and closure evidence were explicit.
Missing approved-label, code-date, and line-clearance prerequisites kept the job blocked and produced a bounded exception note rather than an unsupported start recommendation.
Artwork, supplier, and packaging-specification approval authority were rejected cleanly and the request was routed without widening the lane.
Conflicting lot, pallet, and hold evidence across ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, and traceability systems was treated as a blocker and prevented unsupported progression.
A coding or packaging-quality concern moved the work into hold posture, preserved evidence, and kept final release authority with QA.
Completion was refused until label reconciliation, pallet closure, yield, scrap, and signoff evidence existed, keeping lifecycle state transitions reversible when evidence failed.
Packaging-material substitution pressure was escalated to supplier-quality, procurement, change-control, and QA owners without unsupported component approval.
Low-confidence handling correctly requested release rules, artwork governance, and authoritative record locations rather than guessing.
Conflicting source and tenant policy signals on relabeling and held inventory were summarized and escalated instead of being merged into a false approval answer.
A mid-process miscoding or packaging defect was packaged as an execution exception with explicit rework and release boundaries instead of improvised closure.
Regulatory-label and shipment-release authority were rejected and routed outward while traceability and hold controls remained intact.
Tenant terminology was adapted without losing FDA, GS1, and SQFI baseline controls, lifecycle-state discipline, or explicit assumptions.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to packaging-line execution, label and code-date evidence, lot and pallet traceability, hold and release boundaries, and completion evidence.
Functionality coverage includes all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and all manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities required by the audit contract.
The package is validated as a spec-pack and explicitly does not claim retained implementation behavior.
